parameter.name: enabled
parameter.label: Enable MCP Server
parameter.description: Enable or disable the MCP server endpoint.

parameter.name: registerCloudWebhook
parameter.label: Register openHAB Cloud Webhook
parameter.description: When enabled, registers the /mcp endpoint with the openHAB Cloud service so remote MCP clients (Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, etc.) can reach this server via a stable https://myopenhab.org/api/hooks/<uuid> URL - no port forwarding or reverse proxy needed. Requires the openHAB Cloud add-on to be installed and connected to myopenhab.org.

parameter.name: webhookUrl
parameter.label: Cloud Webhook URL
parameter.description: Public URL assigned by openHAB Cloud once the webhook is registered. Copy this into your remote MCP client (Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, etc.) as the MCP server address. Empty when the Cloud webhook is disabled or the Cloud service isn't connected.

parameter.name: enableFullApiAccess
parameter.label: Enable Full REST API Access
parameter.description: When enabled, the agent gains access to three meta-tools (list_api_endpoints, describe_api_endpoint, call_api) that allow invocation of any openHAB REST endpoint - including destructive operations like deleting items or modifying service configs. The agent's permissions mirror your user. Only enable if you want the agent to have full parity with the REST API. The meta-tools forward the caller's bearer token to /rest/*, so the token's user must have the rights needed for the endpoints the agent calls.

parameter.name: enableScripting
parameter.label: Enable JavaScript Scripting
parameter.description: When enabled, the agent can include JavaScript snippets as rule actions or conditions (type: "script") and gains an execute_script tool that runs JS immediately against openHAB's script engine - useful for dry-running a script before adding it to a scheduled rule. Scripts have the same full system access as any openHAB script (every item, HTTP requests, Exec, OSGi services, rule creation). Requires the openhab-automation-jsscripting add-on.

parameter.name: enableLoggingAccess
parameter.label: Enable Diagnostic Logging Tools
parameter.description: When enabled, the agent gains two tools (get_logs and manage_log_level with actions get/set) for reading recent log entries and adjusting logger verbosity to troubleshoot bindings, rules, or things. Log level changes are sent through the openHAB REST API and require the caller's bearer token to have administrator scope; log reads only require this flag to be on. Verbosity bumps auto-revert after 30 minutes by default so DEBUG won't be left on by accident.

parameter.name: enableUiDesign
parameter.label: Enable Main UI Design Tools
parameter.description: When enabled, the agent gains five tools (list_widgets, describe_widget, get_page_skeleton, manage_ui_component, validate_ui_component) for creating, modifying, and deleting Main UI pages and custom widgets. The tools ship with a curated widget catalog and an embedded reference for the expression language (=items.X.state), action grammar, visibility rules, and slot conventions so the agent produces valid YAML rather than hallucinating component names or prop keys. CRUD writes go through the openHAB REST API at /rest/ui/components/ and require the caller's bearer token to have administrator scope; reads are gated by this flag alone.

parameter.name: enableStaticAssets
parameter.label: Enable Static Asset Management
parameter.description: When enabled, the agent gains manage_static_asset for listing, reading, uploading, and deleting files in $OPENHAB_CONF/html (the folder served at /static/* and used for SVG icons, images, CSS overrides, and other static files the UI references by URL). Useful alongside enableUiDesign so the agent can author an SVG icon or upload an image and then reference it from a page or widget in the same conversation. Writes go directly to disk — there is no REST API to gate this — so the bundle enforces administrator scope itself by probing an admin-gated REST endpoint with the session's bearer token before each call. Filenames are sanitized (including symlinks pointing outside the root), extensions are whitelisted, and uploads are capped at 10 MB per call. Note that .html, .htm, .svg, and .js files served from /static/ execute in the browser, so an agent that writes those becomes an XSS vector against your Main UI; the tool description warns about this but the operator should treat this flag like enableScripting.

parameter.name: exposeUntaggedItems
parameter.label: Expose Untagged Items
parameter.description: Whether to include items that are not part of the semantic model in search results.

parameter.name: maxItemsPerPage
parameter.label: Maximum Items Per Page
parameter.description: Maximum number of items returned per page in paginated tool responses.

parameter.name: resourceCoalesceMs
parameter.label: Resource Notification Coalesce Window
parameter.description: Minimum milliseconds between notifications/resources/updated notifications for the same resource URI. Prevents flooding for chatty items (power meters, CPU load). Set to 0 to disable.
